content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分。你将听到两名学生与讲师讨论恐鸟。首先，
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Section 3. You will hear two students talking about the Moor with the lecturer. First,

content.cn_text: 你们有时间查看第21至26题。
content.en_text: you have some time to look at questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: 现在，请仔细听并回答第21至26题。
content.en_text: Now, listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 26.

content.cn_text: [讲师]: 感谢大家今天来到这里听我介绍恐鸟，这是一种已经
content.en_text: [Lecturer]: Thank you all for coming here today to hear about the Moor, a kind of animal which has been

content.cn_text: 灭绝了很久的动物。首先，我们来看看恐鸟是什么。恐鸟是九种
content.en_text: extinct for a long time. Well, first of all, we look at what the Moor are. The Moor are nine

content.cn_text: 新西兰特有的无翼鸟类。它们是新西兰森林、灌木丛和高山生态系统中
content.en_text: species of flatless birds endemic to New Zealand. They were the dominant herbivores in New Zealand's

content.cn_text: 数千年来占据主导地位的食草动物，但在大约500年前，
content.en_text: forest, shrubland and subalpine ecosystems for thousands of years, but around 500 years ago,

content.cn_text: 它们全部灭绝了。当我提到古代灭绝的动物时，你们可能立刻
content.en_text: they all went extinct. When I mention extinct animals during ancient times, you may immediately

content.cn_text: 想到恐龙，它们在大约6600万年前消失了。用于研究的恐龙化石数量众多，但恐鸟的化石所剩不多。尽管这两种动物今天都吸引着人们。
content.en_text: think of dinosaurs which disappeared around 66 million years ago. Fossils of dinosaurs which were used to study are a large number, but not many of those of Moor remain. Though both animals appeal to people today.

content.cn_text: 用于研究的恐龙化石数量众多，但恐鸟的化石所剩不多。尽管这两种动物
content.en_text: were used to study are a large number, but not many of those of Moor remain. Though both animals

content.cn_text: 今天都吸引着人们。[女学生]: 这样一来，恐鸟听起来就更神秘了，但是先生，我有个问题
content.en_text: appeal to people today. [Female Student]: So, the Moor sound more mysterious now, but sir, I've got a question

content.cn_text: 关于这些无翼鸟。我们如何将它们与其他鸟类区分开来？[讲师]: 问得好。鸟类通常被描述为
content.en_text: about these flatless birds. How can we distinguish them from other birds? [Lecturer]: That's a good question. Birds

content.cn_text: 温血动物，有羽毛和翅膀，通常能飞行，
content.en_text: are commonly characterized by being warm-blooded, having feathers and wings, usually capable of flight,

content.cn_text: 并产卵，而无翼的恐鸟在灭绝前是世界上最大的鸟类。
content.en_text: and laying eggs while the flatless Moor, until their extinction, were the largest birds in the world.

content.cn_text: 它们的头部相对于身体来说比较小，而且它们是唯一没有翅膀的鸟类，
content.en_text: Their heads are relatively small in relation to their bodies, and they are the only wingless birds

content.cn_text: 甚至缺少家族中退化的翅膀和坚固的尾骨。[男学生]: 这真令人印象深刻，
content.en_text: lacking even the vestigial wings and substantial tailbones in their family. [Male Student]: That's impressive,

content.cn_text: 但它们天生就是这样吗？我的意思是，当它们还是雏鸟的时候。[讲师]: 是的，完全正确。所以我们继续
content.en_text: but were they born to be like that? I mean, when they were chicks. [Lecturer]: Yes, absolutely. So let's move

content.cn_text: 谈谈雏鸟。恐鸟的蛋产在巢中，孵化期约为两个月。雏鸟
content.en_text: on to the chicks. The eggs of Moor were laid in nests and incubated for about two months. The

content.cn_text: 孵化后会发育良好，并且可能几乎立即就能离开巢穴
content.en_text: chicks would be well developed upon hatching, and probably would be able to leave the nests to

content.cn_text: 独自觅食。[女学生]: 我听说雄性恐鸟被认为会孵蛋。
content.en_text: feed on their own almost immediately. [Female Student]: I've heard that the male Moor are thought to have incubated

content.cn_text: 那是真的吗？[讲师]: 我认为这有可能。我曾读到过，
content.en_text: the eggs. Is that true? [Lecturer]: I think there is a possibility for that. I've read somewhere that the

content.cn_text: 从蛋壳外表面提取的性别特异性DNA表明这些蛋很可能是由雄性孵化的，但我们仍需查阅更多资料。但我有个问题。
content.en_text: sex-specific DNA recovered from the outer surfaces of eggshells suggested that these eggs were likely to have been hatched by the male, but we still need to consult more. But I have a question.

content.cn_text: 偶尔有人猜测恐鸟仍然活着，因为有人说
content.en_text: There has been some occasional speculation that the Moor were still alive, because someone said

content.cn_text: 他们在19世纪末甚至20世纪在新西兰见过它们。你觉得
content.en_text: they had caught sight of them in New Zealand in the late 19th century or even the 20th. Do you think

content.cn_text: 这可能吗？[男学生]: 我对此并不惊讶，因为这种说法已经有过好几次了。
content.en_text: it's possible? [Male Student]: I'm not amazed by that, since that kind of thing has been claimed several times.

content.cn_text: 但我发现这很有趣，因为从未发现过恐鸟足迹的可靠证据。有位专家
content.en_text: But I find it funny, because no reliable evidence of Moor tracks has ever been found. An expert

content.cn_text: 仍然认为恐鸟存活的可能性极低。[女学生]: 那么恐鸟灭绝的原因是什么呢？
content.en_text: still contend that Moor's survival is extremely unlikely. [Female Student]: So what was the reason for the Moor's

content.cn_text: 灭绝？我想知道是不是全球变暖或者其他与它们生存环境有关的因素。
content.en_text: extinction? I wonder if it was global warming or some other factors related to their living environment.

content.cn_text: [讲师]: 嗯，在新西兰人类定居者到来之前，恐鸟唯一的捕食者
content.en_text: [Lecturer]: Well, before the arrival of human settlers in New Zealand, the Moor's only predator

content.cn_text: 是巨大的哈斯特鹰。然后毛利人大约在公元1300年前抵达，所有恐鸟
content.en_text: was the massive hearse eagle. Then the Maori arrived sometime before CE-1300, and all Moor

content.cn_text: 属很快就因狩猎而灭绝了。多么可怕的事情。
content.en_text: Genera was soon driven to extinction by hunting. What a horrible thing.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 现在，请听并回答第27-30题。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: Now, listen and answer questions 27-30.

content.cn_text: [讲师]: 好的，现在我们来看看一些恐鸟物种的特征。南岛巨型恐鸟
content.en_text: [Lecturer]: All right, now let's look at the features of some species of Moor. The South Island giant Moor

content.cn_text: 可能是已知最高的鸟类，而九种恐鸟中第二高的是
content.en_text: may have been the tallest birds ever known, and the second tallest of the nine Moor species

content.cn_text: 北岛巨型恐鸟，雌性在体重和身高上都明显大于雄性。
content.en_text: is the North Island giant Moor, with the females being markedly larger than males, both in

content.cn_text: 体重和身高。[男学生]: 我听说恐鸟中最小的是海岸恐鸟。
content.en_text: weight and height. [Male Student]: And I've heard that the smallest of the Moor birds are the coastal Moor.

content.cn_text: 对吗？[讲师]: 是的，你对了，你以前听说过其他种类的恐鸟吗？
content.en_text: Is that right? [Lecturer]: Yes, you were right, and have you heard about any other kind of Moor before?

content.cn_text: [女学生]: 我知道凤头恐鸟。蛋可能比其他恐鸟的更大。
content.en_text: [Female Student]: I know the Crested Moor. The Eggstay Lake may be larger than others.

content.cn_text: [讲师]: 由于它们主要生活在南岛偏远的内陆地区，它们的化石很少或
content.en_text: [Lecturer]: As they mainly lived in the remote interior of the southern island, their fossils are rare or

content.cn_text: 在考古遗址中缺失，并且尚未发现任何蛋的遗骸。[女学生]: 有没有恐鸟物种
content.en_text: absent in archaeological sites, and no egg remains have yet been identified. [Female Student]: Are there any species

content.cn_text: 有更多化石的？[讲师]: 是的，当然。大量
content.en_text: of Moor that have got more fossils? [Lecturer]: Yes, of course. A considerable amount of remains of the

content.cn_text: 粗腿恐鸟的遗骸存在，因为它们的栖息地保存良好。
content.en_text: stout-legged Moor exist, due to the well-preserved properties of their habitat.

content.cn_text: 它们的头骨显示相对较差的视力，良好的嗅觉和非常短的喙。
content.en_text: Their skulls reveal relatively bad vision, a good sense of smell, and a very short bill.

content.cn_text: 接着是东部恐鸟。它们以拥有非常长而窄的气管而著称，
content.en_text: Then there is the Eastern Moor. They were remarkable in having very long and narrow wind pipes,

content.cn_text: 这可能使它们能够发出比其他恐鸟更响亮、更有共鸣的叫声，
content.en_text: which probably enabled them to make louder, more resonant calls than those of other Moor,

content.cn_text: 并拥有最强的发声能力，这样它们在森林里或夜晚无法看到彼此时也能交流。
content.en_text: and had the greatest vocal abilities, so they could communicate when they could not see each other

content.cn_text: 在森林里或夜晚。它们在觅食时除了声音之外还利用了多种感官，
content.en_text: in the forest or at night. They used a range of senses, apart from sound in their search for food,

content.cn_text: 例如它们的嗅觉和视觉。
content.en_text: such as their sense of smell and vision.

content.cn_text: [旁白]: 第三部分到此结束。现在你们有半分钟时间检查答案。
content.en_text: [Narrator]: That is the end of section 3. You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter A, B or C
groups.title: Question 21-26.

items.title: What is the thing that makes the Moa similar to dinosaur?

options.title: A
options.content: Both are of interest to the public.

options.title: B
options.content: Both are extinct at similar time

options.title: C
options.content: Both left lots of fossil remains

items.title: What is the difference between Moa and other birds?
What’s the special feature of their chicks?

options.title: A
options.content: They never return to the nests.

options.title: B
options.content: Most of them die within two months after birth.

options.title: C
options.content: They can find food by themselves.

items.title: What is the tutor’s opinion on male hatching the eggs?

options.title: A
options.content: He doubts whether it is true or possible.

options.title: B
options.content: He thinks it may be true.

options.title: C
options.content: He can say with certainty that it is true.

items.title: What is the male student’s response after hearing some people see a Moa recently?
Why did the Moa become extinct?

groups.desc: Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter A–F
